description: Bundled fuel system cards covering tank cells, boost pumps, vents, low fuel indications, wing tanks, and crossfeed cues.

cards.front: How many cells are in the DHC-6 main fuel tank group?
cards.back: There are eight fuel cells in the main tank group, arranged as four cells in each tank group.

cards.front: Which fuel cells are the collector cells?
cards.back: Cells 4 and 5 are the collector cells.

cards.front: How many boost pumps are installed in each fuel tank group?
cards.back: Two boost pumps are located in each fuel tank group.

cards.front: What is normal pressure from the main tank boost pumps?
At what pressure may a boost pump fail light illuminate?
cards.back: At pressures below approximately 2 to 3 PSI.

cards.front: How are fuel boost pumps cooled and lubricated?
cards.back: They are cooled and lubricated by fuel only.

cards.front: Why does the failed boost pump checklist require pulling the circuit breaker?
cards.back: A seized pump may overheat if left powered, creating a theoretical fuel tank fire risk.

cards.front: What is the normal pressure for optional wing tank boost pumps and why is it higher?
cards.back: Wing tank pumps normally provide about 23 to 24 PSI so fuel is preferentially used from the wing tanks when selected.

cards.front: What fuel quantity does a normal low fuel light indicate?
cards.back: Approximately 75 lb remaining in the forward tank and/or 110 lb remaining in the aft tank.

cards.front: What can happen if a fuel vent becomes plugged or iced over?
cards.back: A blocked vent can lead to a collapsed fuel cell.

cards.front: What may be indicated if one tank level drops much faster than the other after refuelling?
cards.back: The crossfeed valve may have failed in the open position; fuel balance should be managed using crossfeed selections as appropriate.
